summary: The review of "Contract Example 3.pdf" reveals a generally moderate risk posture, with several clauses flagged for further attention. Key issues include deviations from UoA's Preferred Contracting Positions in areas such as liability, intellectual property, confidentiality, and dispute resolution, with some clauses requiring escalation to specialized teams (e.g., Tax, IP, Risk). Additionally, numerous clauses fall outside the scope of documented UoA policies, necessitating independent review. The next step is to escalate flagged clauses to the appropriate UoA teams for alignment confirmation and to address any material risks before finalizing the agreement.

flags.clause_title: 5 LIABILITY AND INSURANCE
flags.snippet: 5 LIABILITY AND INSURANCE
flags.rationale: The clause text does not provide sufficient detail to confirm alignment with UoA Position #POS-07 on Liability Limitations and Exclusions. Specifically, it lacks explicit mention of a liability cap, exclusions for consequential or indirect losses, and carve-outs for fraud, wilful default, personal injury, or confidentiality obligations. Additionally, the clause does not address insurance coverage requirements as outlined in UoA Position #POS-09. This ambiguity triggers escalation to the Research Grants and Contracts Delegations for liability and the Risk Office for insurance.
flags.text: 5 LIABILITY AND INSURANCE

flags.clause_title: 5.1 The CONTRACTOR indemnifies [redacted] and will keep [redacted] indemnified
flags.snippet: 5.1 The CONTRACTOR indemnifies [redacted] and will keep [redacted] indemnified against any loss, damage or expenses (including legal costs and any taxes, levies, withholdings and deductions due from any payment to the C…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses indemnities, which are covered under UoA Position POS-08. The clause materially deviates from the Preferred and Acceptable tiers. Specifically, it does not limit the indemnity to damage or injury caused by the CONTRACTOR's negligent acts or omissions or wilful misconduct, nor does it explicitly include a liability cap or a proportionate reduction for contributory negligence. Additionally, it includes broad coverage for taxes, levies, and penalties, which is not aligned with UoA's positions. This clause requires escalation to the Research Contract Delegations for review.
flags.text: 5.1 The CONTRACTOR indemnifies [redacted] and will keep [redacted] indemnified against any loss, damage or expenses (including legal costs and any taxes, levies, withholdings and deductions due from any payment to the CONTRACTOR and any interest and penalties imposed thereon) of any nature which may arise from breach of this Agreement by the CONTRACTOR or from or as a consequence of the provision of the Services or any act or omission of the CONTRACTOR, except to the extent that [redacted] is responsible for the loss, damage or expense.

flags.clause_title: 5.2 Where the Specific Terms contain insurance details, the CONTRACTOR shall
flags.snippet: 5.2 Where the Specific Terms contain insurance details, the CONTRACTOR shall maintain the insurance set out in the Specific Terms, for no lesser amount than that specified and for a term expiring no earlier than six yea…
flags.rationale: The clause requires the CONTRACTOR to maintain insurance for a term expiring no earlier than six years after the Completion Date or termination. This materially deviates from UoA Position #POS-09, which prefers insurance cover sufficient to conduct the work without reference to explicit policy limits or extended durations. The six-year post-completion requirement is excessive and outside the acceptable tier. Escalation to the Risk Office is required to assess whether this extended insurance obligation aligns with UoA's risk tolerance and policy limits.
flags.text: 5.2 Where the Specific Terms contain insurance details, the CONTRACTOR shall maintain the insurance set out in the Specific Terms, for no lesser amount than that specified and for a term expiring no earlier than six years after the Completion Date or the date of termination of this Agreement. At [redacted]'s request the CONTRACTOR shall produce certificates evidencing the currency of the insurance and that the cover meets the requirements of the Specific Terms.

flags.clause_title: 8. SUSPENSION OR TERMINATION
flags.snippet: 8. SUSPENSION OR TERMINATION
flags.rationale: The clause text does not provide sufficient detail to confirm alignment with UoA Position #POS-19 on Termination. The clause must explicitly address mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, UoA's right to terminate for non-payment, and accrued rights and benefits. Additionally, termination costs and uncancellable obligations must be covered. Without these specifics, the clause falls outside the Acceptable tier and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts.
flags.text: 8. SUSPENSION OR TERMINATION

flags.clause_title: 9. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
flags.snippet: 9. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
flags.rationale: The clause text does not provide sufficient detail to confirm alignment with UoA Position #POS-18. The Preferred tier requires specific elements: good faith discussions, escalation to senior management, mediation before arbitration/litigation, proceedings in English, and allowance for urgent interlocutory court action. None of these elements are evident in the clause snippet provided. Escalation to Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts or General Counsel is required to ensure compliance with UoA's dispute resolution standards.
flags.text: 9. DISPUTE RESOLUTION

flags.clause_title: 2.1 [redacted] shall pay the CONTRACTOR the Fee and any agreed Disbursements
flags.snippet: 2.1 [redacted] shall pay the CONTRACTOR the Fee and any agreed Disbursements in the amounts and at the times specified in this Agreement. [redacted] may however deduct from the Fee any withholding payments, such as non-resident withholding tax, that is required by law to deduct.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses taxation, specifically withholding tax, which is a UoA-sensitive topic under Position #POS-06. While the clause does not explicitly contradict the Preferred Position ('NZ contracts: costs presented as excluding GST; overseas contracts: costs excluding any applicable taxes and duties, payable by the Client on any taxable supplies'), it does not confirm alignment either. The clause allows for deductions of withholding tax as required by law, which is reasonable but requires confirmation that this aligns with UoA's tax compliance practices. Escalation to the Tax and Compliance team is recommended if offshore tax incorporation is required.
flags.text: 2.1 [redacted] shall pay the CONTRACTOR the Fee and any agreed Disbursements in the amounts and at the times specified in this Agreement. [redacted] may however deduct from the Fee any withholding payments, such as non-resident withholding tax, that is required by law to deduct.

flags.clause_title: 3 CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
flags.snippet: 3 CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the topic of Confidentiality, which is covered under UoA Position #POS-11. However, the clause text provided does not include sufficient detail to confirm alignment with the Preferred or Acceptable tiers. Specifically, it is unclear whether the definition of confidential information excludes public, independently developed, or previously known information, or whether UoA retains the ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates. Additionally, the duration of confidentiality obligations is not specified. This ambiguity necessitates further review by the Contract Manager to ensure compliance with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions.
flags.text: 3 CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

flags.clause_title: 3.1 The CONTRACTOR shall not disclose to any third party at any time the terms
flags.snippet: 3.1 The CONTRACTOR shall not disclose to any third party at any time the terms of this Agreement or at any time thereafter any confidential or proprietary information belonging to [redacted] or any information which rel…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses confidentiality but does not align fully with UoA's Preferred Position under POS-11. Specifically, it lacks exclusions for information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed. Additionally, the clause imposes confidentiality obligations 'at any time thereafter,' which exceeds the Acceptable tier's 5–7 year post-termination/expiry limit. While it allows disclosure to employees and officers on a 'need to know' basis with equivalent confidentiality obligations, it does not explicitly permit UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates. This deviation places the clause in the Acceptable tier, but it requires confirmation and potential adjustment to align with UoA's Preferred Position.
flags.text: 3.1 The CONTRACTOR shall not disclose to any third party at any time the terms of this Agreement or at any time thereafter any confidential or proprietary information belonging to [redacted] or any information which relates to its business affairs or unless such information has become public knowledge without the default of the CONTRACTOR. The CONTRACTOR may disclose such confidential or proprietary information on a strictly "need to know" basis to its employees, and if a company its officers also, provided that all employees and officers are first advised of the confidential nature of the information and they are subject to written confidentiality obligations and restrictions on use at least equivalent to those in this Agreement.

flags.clause_title: 4 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
flags.snippet: 4 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, which is explicitly outside the scope of UoA Contracting Positions and must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review. This aligns with UoA Position #POS-IP, which mandates referral of all IP clauses regardless of wording.
flags.text: 4 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

flags.clause_title: 4.1 Copyright and all other intellectual property rights in all documents,
flags.snippet: 4.1 Copyright and all other intellectual property rights in all documents, computer programmes, data, information and other things capable of ownership commissioned and paid for by [redacted] as part of the Services or …
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, which is explicitly outside the scope of UoA Contracting Positions and must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review. This aligns with UoA Position #POS-IP, which mandates referral of all IP clauses regardless of wording.
flags.text: 4.1 Copyright and all other intellectual property rights in all documents, computer programmes, data, information and other things capable of ownership commissioned and paid for by [redacted] as part of the Services or created or developed in the course of providing the Services shall vest in [redacted].

flags.clause_title: 4.2 To enable [redacted] to fully and reasonably utilize the Services the
flags.snippet: 4.2 To enable [redacted] to fully and reasonably utilize the Services the CONTRACTOR grants to [redacted] an irrevocable, royalty free, non-exclusive, transferable license to use and, if reasonably required, reproduce a...
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, which is explicitly outside the scope of UoA Contracting Positions and must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review. The clause involves licensing of IP created independently of the Services but used as part of the Services, which requires UniServices' expertise to assess compliance with UoA's IP policies.
flags.text: 4.2 To enable [redacted] to fully and reasonably utilize the Services the CONTRACTOR grants to [redacted] an irrevocable, royalty free, non-exclusive, transferable license to use and, if reasonably required, reproduce all documents, computer programmes, data, information and other things in which the CONTRACTOR holds the intellectual property rights and that have been acquired or created independently of the Services but provided or used as part of the Services.

flags.clause_title: 8.1 [REDACTED] may at any time on written notice to the CONTRACTOR suspend the
flags.snippet: 8.1 [REDACTED] may at any time on written notice to the CONTRACTOR suspend the provision of all or part of the Services or terminate this Agreement by written notice to the CONTRACTOR. The CONTRACTOR shall use its best
flags.rationale: This clause addresses termination, a UoA-sensitive topic under Position #POS-19. While it provides for termination with notice and payment of fees/disbursements up to the termination date (if not due to breach by the contractor), it also allows for termination 'at any time' without specifying mutuality or material breach. This deviates from the Preferred Position, which requires mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, and clear rights for UoA to terminate for non-payment. However, it does not explicitly conflict with the Acceptable Position, which allows termination for convenience in certain cases (e.g., consulting contracts) provided termination costs are covered. The clause does not explicitly address accrued rights or uncancellable obligations, which are part of the Preferred Position. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, is recommended for further review.
flags.text: 8.1 [REDACTED] may at any time on written notice to the CONTRACTOR suspend the provision of all or part of the Services or terminate this Agreement by written notice to the CONTRACTOR. The CONTRACTOR shall use its best endeavours to immediately cease providing the Services, and shall arrange for the orderly cessation of Services and shall not incur any further expenditure on the Services. Unless such suspension or termination has been ordered by [REDACTED] with cause, or as a result of breach by the CONTRACTOR, [REDACTED] shall pay the CONTRACTOR the amount of Fees and any Disbursements incurred to the date of such suspension or termination.

flags.clause_title: 12.4 If either party identifies any hazard or risk associated with the
flags.snippet: 12.4 If either party identifies any hazard or risk associated with the carrying out and completion of the Services, that party shall notify the other party of such hazard or risk as soon as practicable. In the event that the CONTRACTOR becomes aware of unforeseen safety risk that is likely to affect the CONTRACTOR’s ability to undertake the Services, then the CONTRACTOR shall notify [REDACTED] as soon as practicable and the parties shall consult in good faith to agree an extension of time, variation to the Services and/or variation to the remuneration payable accordingly.
flags.rationale: The clause touches on the UoA-sensitive topic of Variations and Extensions (UoA Position #POS-21). While it provides for consultation and agreement on extensions or variations, it does not explicitly require that such changes be negotiated and executed in writing prior to expiry of the current Agreement, as per the Preferred Position. However, it does not contradict the Acceptable Position, which allows extensions negotiated and executed in writing after expiry. The clause is flagged as amber because it aligns with the Acceptable tier but does not fully meet the Preferred Position.
flags.text: 12.4 If either party identifies any hazard or risk associated with the carrying out and completion of the Services, that party shall notify the other party of such hazard or risk as soon as practicable. In the event that the CONTRACTOR becomes aware of unforeseen safety risk that is likely to affect the CONTRACTOR’s ability to undertake the Services, then the CONTRACTOR shall notify [REDACTED] as soon as practicable and the parties shall consult in good faith to agree an extension of time, variation to the Services and/or variation to the remuneration payable accordingly.

flags.clause_title: If a dispute arises out of or related to this Agreement [REDACTED] and the
flags.snippet: If a dispute arises out of or related to this Agreement [REDACTED] and the CONTRACTOR shall first endeavour, in good faith and without delay, to resolve the dispute by mediation. In the event the dispute is not settled by mediation within 20 days, or such other period as agreed to in writing by [REDACTED] and the CONTRACTOR, the dispute will be referred to arbitration under the Arbitration Act 1996. There will be a single arbitrator and the arbitration will be held in Wellington, New Zealand, and the CONTRACTOR will abide by any decision of the arbitrator. The cost of arbitration proceedings shall be borne as directed by the arbitrator.
flags.rationale: The clause aligns partially with UoA Position #POS-18 on Dispute Resolution. It includes good faith mediation and arbitration, which are consistent with the preferred tier. However, it does not explicitly mention escalation to senior management before mediation or the ability to pursue urgent interlocutory action through courts, which are key elements of the preferred position. The clause does not fall outside the acceptable tier, as it specifies arbitration under New Zealand law and does not prevent urgent court action. Escalation to the Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts or General Counsel is recommended for confirmation.
flags.text: If a dispute arises out of or related to this Agreement [REDACTED] and the CONTRACTOR shall first endeavour, in good faith and without delay, to resolve the dispute by mediation. In the event the dispute is not settled by mediation within 20 days, or such other period as agreed to in writing by [REDACTED] and the CONTRACTOR, the dispute will be referred to arbitration under the Arbitration Act 1996. There will be a single arbitrator and the arbitration will be held in Wellington, New Zealand, and the CONTRACTOR will abide by any decision of the arbitrator. The cost of arbitration proceedings shall be borne as directed by the arbitrator.

flags.clause_title: 1 THE SERVICES
flags.snippet: 1 THE SERVICES
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the topic of 'Services,' which is not explicitly covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or associated templates. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions.
flags.text: 1 THE SERVICES

flags.clause_title: 1.1 The CONTRACTOR shall perform the Services in accordance with the
flags.snippet: 1.1 The CONTRACTOR shall perform the Services in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement. If the CONTRACTOR becomes aware of anything that will materially affect the scope or timing of the Services, the CONTRAC…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the contractor's obligation to perform services and notify of material changes to scope or timing. This topic is not explicitly covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or tied to any specific policy. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and is flagged as blue.
flags.text: 1.1 The CONTRACTOR shall perform the Services in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement. If the CONTRACTOR becomes aware of anything that will materially affect the scope or timing of the Services, the CONTRACTOR must immediately notify [redacted].

flags.clause_title: 1.2 In providing the Services the CONTRACTOR shall exercise the degree of
flags.snippet: 1.2 In providing the Services the CONTRACTOR shall exercise the degree of skill, care, diligence and foresight reasonably expected from a skilled and experienced provider. The CONTRACTOR is hereby given notice that [red
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the standard of care, reliance on the contractor's performance, record-keeping, and rectification of defective services. These topics are not explicitly covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or associated escalation protocols. As such, the clause falls outside the scope of the documented positions and is flagged as blue.
flags.text: 1.2 In providing the Services the CONTRACTOR shall exercise the degree of skill, care, diligence and foresight reasonably expected from a skilled and experienced provider. The CONTRACTOR is hereby given notice that [redacted] is relying on the accuracy, competence and completeness of the Services and upon the lawful performance of the Services by the CONTRACTOR. The CONTRACTOR shall keep records of information and material gathered or created in performing the Services. If any Services provided by the CONTRACTOR are defective or wholly or partly underperformed then [redacted] may require the CONTRACTOR to rectify, perform or re-perform these Services at the CONTRACTOR's expense.

flags.clause_title: 1.3 Any Key Personnel specified in the Specific Terms shall give first priority
flags.snippet: 1.3 Any Key Personnel specified in the Specific Terms shall give first priority to the Services, and shall not become involved in any other project or matter that may adversely affect their ability to provide the Servic…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the obligations and replacement of Key Personnel, a topic not covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. As there is no guidance or template for this topic, it falls outside the scope of the existing policy framework.
flags.text: 1.3 Any Key Personnel specified in the Specific Terms shall give first priority to the Services, and shall not become involved in any other project or matter that may adversely affect their ability to provide the Services. The written approval of [redacted] shall be obtained by the CONTRACTOR before any such Key Personnel can be replaced or substituted.

flags.clause_title: 1.4 The CONTRACTOR shall apply for and obtain all consents required (including
flags.snippet: 1.4 The CONTRACTOR shall apply for and obtain all consents required (including statutory consents and, if applicable, visas and work permits) for providing the Services. The CONTRACTOR shall notify [redacted] as soon as practicable upon it being apparent or suspected that any required consent is unavailable or if consent cannot be obtained by the date by which it is required.
flags.rationale: The topic of obtaining consents, including statutory consents, visas, and work permits, is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. As such, this clause falls outside the scope of the documented policy and cannot be evaluated against a specific tier.

flags.clause_title: 8.2 Upon completion of the Services or sooner termination or suspension of
flags.snippet: 8.2 Upon completion of the Services or sooner termination or suspension of this Agreement the CONTRACTOR shall deliver to [REDACTED] all documents, records, materials (whether completed or not, or in physical or electronic form) gathered, prepared or provided in performing the Services and shall return to [REDACTED] all materials, data and information provided by [REDACTED] to the CONTRACTOR to enable performance of the Services.
flags.rationale: The clause primarily addresses the return of materials and records upon completion, termination, or suspension of the agreement. While it mentions termination, it does not directly address the termination rights, obligations, or costs as outlined in UoA Position #POS-19. The clause's focus is on operational procedures rather than termination terms, and this specific topic is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Position or template. Therefore, it falls outside the scope of the Positions document and is flagged as blue.
flags.standard_ref: Not covered by UoA Position or template
flags.text: 8.2 Upon completion of the Services or sooner termination or suspension of this Agreement the CONTRACTOR shall deliver to [REDACTED] all documents, records, materials (whether completed or not, or in physical or electronic form) gathered, prepared or provided in performing the Services and shall return to [REDACTED] all materials, data and information provided by [REDACTED] to the CONTRACTOR to enable performance of the Services.

flags.snippet: 10. INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR

flags.clause_title: 10.1 In performing the Services, the CONTRACTOR shall operate, and have the
flags.snippet: 10.1 In performing the Services, the CONTRACTOR shall operate, and have the status of, an independent contractor and shall not act as, or be an agent of [REDACTED] in any way. As an independent contractor, the CONTRACTOR shall have the right to determine how the Services will be accomplished except to the extent the scope, quality and timing of the Services are specified in this Agreement.

flags.clause_title: 12.1 The parties are responsible for meeting their respective health and
flags.snippet: 12.1 The parties are responsible for meeting their respective health and safety obligations at law and must as far as reasonably practicable consult, co-operate with and co-ordinate activities with each other and any su…

flags.text: 12.1 The parties are responsible for meeting their respective health and safety obligations at law and must as far as reasonably practicable consult, co-operate with and co-ordinate activities with each other and any subcontractors or other parties. The parties shall comply with their work engagement, participation and representation obligations.

flags.clause_title: 12.2 Prior to commencement of the Services, [REDACTED] shall, to the extent
flags.snippet: 12.2 Prior to commencement of the Services, [REDACTED] shall, to the extent necessary (as to be determined at [REDACTED]s discretion), inform the CONTRACTOR of any work it considers to be hazardous and any measures to
flags.rationale: The clause addresses health and safety obligations, including hazard identification and management plans, which are not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the Positions document and cannot be evaluated against a specific tier.
flags.text: 12.2 Prior to commencement of the Services, [REDACTED] shall, to the extent necessary (as to be determined at [REDACTED]’s discretion), inform the CONTRACTOR of any work it considers to be hazardous and any measures to manage the hazards and risks that are required by regulation, by industry practice, or by [REDACTED] (acting reasonably), including, where applicable, as set out in [REDACTED] health and safety plans. To the extent required by [REDACTED], the CONTRACTOR will prepare a health and safety plan for the work (the “Plan”). This must identify all potential hazards and risks and the measures to manage those hazards and risks, including any identified by [REDACTED]. The CONTRACTOR will provide the Plan to [REDACTED] for their review and feedback and will then revise it to address that feedback.

flags.clause_title: 12.3 The parties will comply with the Plan and will keep it up to date as
flags.snippet: 12.3 The parties will comply with the Plan and will keep it up to date as required. The CONTRACTOR will take all reasonably practicable steps to comply with any other reasonable health and safety request made by [REDACTED] while performing the Services. Should either party be in default of any of its health and safety obligations while performing the Services and the other party reasonably considers that it is unsafe for the CONTRACTOR to continue to perform the Services, the other party may require the suspension of performance of the Services until the default is rectified.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses health and safety obligations and suspension of services due to non-compliance, which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and is flagged as blue.
flags.text: 12.3 The parties will comply with the Plan and will keep it up to date as required. Should either party be in default of any of its health and safety obligations while performing the Services and the other party reasonably considers that it is unsafe for the CONTRACTOR to continue to perform the Services, the other party may require the suspension of performance of the Services until the default is rectified.

flags.clause_title: 12.5 The CONTRACTOR will take all reasonably practicable steps to assure
flags.snippet: 12.5 The CONTRACTOR will take all reasonably practicable steps to assure itself that, from a health and safety perspective, each subcontractor is competent to undertake the work required of it before such subcontractor commences work, and will provide [REDACTED] with any information requested to assist [REDACTED] in assessing the suitability, or otherwise, of the CONTRACTOR as part of its approval of the contractor. The CONTRACTOR accepts responsibility for ensuring that all subcontractors are aware of and comply with the site-specific health and safety plan and any other reasonable health and safety request made by [REDACTED].

flags.text: 15.2 No communication under this Agreement is effective until received. Communications are, however, deemed to be received: (a) if sent by letter, the third business day after posting; (b) if sent by facsimile, on the business day on which it was sent or, if despatched after 5.00 pm or on a non-business day, on the next business day after the date of despatch; (c) if delivered, when delivered by the sender; (d) if by e-mail, the earlier of the sender’s receipt of confirmation of successful delivery, or one working day after dispatch being when the relevant e-mail first leaves the sender’s network for delivery to the recipient’s address, provided the sender does not receive any indication of the failure, or delay, in delivery within one day after dispatch.

flags.clause_title: 2.2 The CONTRACTOR shall issue an invoice to [redacted] for payments due and
flags.snippet: 2.2 The CONTRACTOR shall issue an invoice to [redacted] for payments due and shall include a breakdown of costs and Disbursements (copies of receipts for Disbursements will be made available to [redacted] on request). W…
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA's preferred position on Payment Terms as outlined in UoA Position #POS-15. It specifies that all payments shall be made on the 20th of the month following receipt of a valid invoice, which matches the preferred standard. Additionally, the inclusion of valid GST tax invoice requirements aligns with standard financial practices.
flags.text: 2.2 The CONTRACTOR shall issue an invoice to [redacted] for payments due and shall include a breakdown of costs and Disbursements (copies of receipts for Disbursements will be made available to [redacted] on request). Where the Services are chargeable with New Zealand Goods and Services Tax (GST) the invoice shall be a valid GST tax invoice. Disbursements will be billed at cost. All payments shall be made by [redacted] on the 20th of the month following receipt of a valid invoice. In the event the whole or part of it is disputed by [redacted] then [redacted] may withhold payment of the disputed amount until the dispute is resolved in accordance with clause [redacted].
